Tests the behavior of the `arguments` object in a function with extra parameters.

# Purpose
This code defines a function `f_arg` that takes two parameters, `x` and `y`, and returns the `arguments` object, which contains all arguments passed to the function. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that accessing the fourth element of the `arguments` object, `f_arg(1,2,3)[3]`, returns `undefined`, as only three arguments are passed to the function. This script demonstrates the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript, specifically how it captures all arguments passed to a function, regardless of the defined parameters.
